descriptionEn: Name means "wrestling." He is the fifth son of Jacob through Rachel's handmaid Bilhah and is so named because Rachel "wrestled" with her sister over having children and prevailed.
articleEn: A son of Jacob and of Rachel's maid—servant Bilhah. The name was given him by Rachel to indicate how she had "wrestled" in contest with her sister to obtain a child as a mark of God's favor (Gen 30:7, 8). He was the father of four sons and the ancestor of the tribe that bore his name (Gen 46:24 Num 26:48, 49).

verses.line: And Bilhah Rachel's maid conceived again, and bare Jacob a second son.
And Rachel said, With great wrestlings have I wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed: and she called his name Naphtali.

images.caption: Symbol of the tribe of Naphtali by the S.S. Teacher's Edition: The Holy Bible. New York: Henry Frowde, Publisher to the University of Oxford, 1896.
